title: The Tide Is Turning Against Russia
content: With Ukraine reclaiming territory, mounting drone strikes deep in Russian territory, and draining Russia’s coffers, Vladimir Putin would do well to seek a negotiated freeze to the conflict. But there is no guarantee that he will, not least because he might not grasp the situation Russia is in.
snippet: With Ukraine reclaiming territory, mounting drone strikes deep in Russian territory, and draining Russia’s coffers, Vladimir Putin would do well to seek a negotiated freeze to the conflict. But there
creator: Sergei Guriev
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Managing Sino-American Interdependence
content: At their summit last month, US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping seemed to recognize that they are in the same boat. If either the United States or China tries to cripple or decouple from the other, it will harm its own firms, consumers, financial markets, and innovation ecosystem.
snippet: At their summit last month, US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping seemed to recognize that they are in the same boat. If either the United States or China tries to cripple or
creator: Qiyuan Xu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Who Gains in an AI-Supercharged Economy?
content: If AI delivers as promised and fundamentally transforms the economy, we will have moved from the current investment phase, where AI builders dominate, to one where AI users will reap the greatest reward. That is what happens every time an innovation becomes a general-purpose technology.
snippet: If AI delivers as promised and fundamentally transforms the economy, we will have moved from the current investment phase, where AI builders dominate, to one where AI users will reap the greatest
creator: Joseph H. Davis
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Development in a Time of Disruption
content: Once firms and economies ascend to market dominance, they often kick away the ladder behind them and erect barriers to market entry to stymie would-be challengers. But developing countries can and do leapfrog incumbent technologies, and conditions for doing so may be particularly favorable now.
snippet: Once firms and economies ascend to market dominance, they often kick away the ladder behind them and erect barriers to market entry to stymie would-be challengers. But developing countries can and do
creator: Keun Lee
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Trump’s Un-American Capitalism
content: The current US administration's openly corrupt style of crony capitalism is the antithesis of the institutional foundation on which the US economy was built. The winners in the new oligarchic competition are not those who make the best products, but rather those who are best at flattering the mad king.
snippet: The current US administration's openly corrupt style of crony capitalism is the antithesis of the institutional foundation on which the US economy was built. The winners in the new oligarchic
creator: Joseph E. Stiglitz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Can the Manchester Model Save Britain?
content: Following a decades-long effort to lay the foundations for economic dynamism, Greater Manchester has become the UK’s fastest-growing region. While the Manchester model cannot be precisely replicated at the national level, it does carry important lessons for outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer's likely successor.
snippet: Following a decades-long effort to lay the foundations for economic dynamism, Greater Manchester has become the UK’s fastest-growing region. While the Manchester model cannot be precisely replicated
creator: Diane Coyle
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Trillionaires Are Really Made
content: Some may argue that the world’s first trillionaire is a story of technological triumph and a win for American capitalism. But markets have not bet on cutting-edge technology or a company too big to fail, but rather on the idea of a mega-monopolist with wealth and influence so vast that the US government will not allow him to fail.
snippet: Some may argue that the world’s first trillionaire is a story of technological triumph and a win for American capitalism. But markets have not bet on cutting-edge technology or a company too big to
creator: Nabil Ahmed
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Cities Show How Climate Action Makes Life Better
content: Cities have shown that a combination of adaptation measures and energy efficiency can save lives, improve well-being, reduce energy costs, and create conditions for sustainable growth. A new partnership seeks to help mayors and city leaders scale up these efforts through technical and financial assistance.
snippet: Cities have shown that a combination of adaptation measures and energy efficiency can save lives, improve well-being, reduce energy costs, and create conditions for sustainable growth. A new
creator: Michael R. Bloomberg
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Myth of Global Chaos
content: Today’s seemingly nonstop international tumult is not as unintelligible as it seems. What pundits lazily describe as chaos is the culmination of developments that were long in the making and arrived at a time when the international system was no longer able to prevent or mitigate geopolitical shocks.
snippet: Today’s seemingly nonstop international tumult is not as unintelligible as it seems. What pundits lazily describe as chaos is the culmination of developments that were long in the making and arrived
creator: Zaki Laïdi
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Microfinance Debate Is Missing the Point
content: For too long, policymakers, economists, and investors have focused on whether microfinance actually helps people, reducing a complex issue to a meaningless yes-or-no verdict. The most important questions concern how loans are designed, delivered, and regulated, who receives them, and what they are used for.
snippet: For too long, policymakers, economists, and investors have focused on whether microfinance actually helps people, reducing a complex issue to a meaningless yes-or-no verdict. The most important
creator: Sophie Sirtaine
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Turning Latin America’s Resilience Into Growth
content: Economic stabilization has been a significant achievement for Latin America and the Caribbean, a region with a long history of financial crises. But these countries need a well-sequenced reform agenda to attract private investment and create the growth engine that will translate hard-won gains into higher living standards.
snippet: Economic stabilization has been a significant achievement for Latin America and the Caribbean, a region with a long history of financial crises. But these countries need a well-sequenced reform agenda
creator: Ilan Goldfajn
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The World Cup vs. the Extremists
content: Germany’s 2026 World Cup team, like so many others in the tournament, shows that loving one’s country and welcoming newcomers are not incompatible impulses. Although activists on the far left and the far right would have us believe otherwise, most ordinary people implicitly recognize this.
snippet: Germany’s 2026 World Cup team, like so many others in the tournament, shows that loving one’s country and welcoming newcomers are not incompatible impulses. Although activists on the far left and the
creator: Daron Acemoglu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Beyond Brexit—and Back to Europe
content: Those who follow only intergovernmental negotiations or debates in the media might think that nothing has changed in the decade since the United Kingdom's voters decided to leave the European Union. Yet new polling finds that large majorities on both sides of the English Channel now support re-engagement.
snippet: Those who follow only intergovernmental negotiations or debates in the media might think that nothing has changed in the decade since the United Kingdom's voters decided to leave the European Union.
creator: Mark Leonard
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Trump’s March of Folly in Iran
content: In promoting his “deal” with Iran, US President Donald Trump has tried to dress up a humiliating defeat as a victory. While it’s tempting to blame this outcome on incompetent leadership, it stems more from the policies and institutions that have allowed entertainers and profiteers to rise to power.
snippet: In promoting his “deal” with Iran, US President Donald Trump has tried to dress up a humiliating defeat as a victory. While it’s tempting to blame this outcome on incompetent leadership, it stems more
creator: Timothy Snyder
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Alan Greenspan’s Mixed Legacy
content: The longtime Federal Reserve chair inferred from past crises that lightly regulated markets, while prone to excesses, could right themselves sufficiently to avoid imperiling the financial system and the economy. The correct lesson would have been that markets require strict regulation, and that competent technocrats are essential.
snippet: The longtime Federal Reserve chair inferred from past crises that lightly regulated markets, while prone to excesses, could right themselves sufficiently to avoid imperiling the financial system and
creator: Barry Eichengreen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Will AI Yield Abundance Without Purpose?
content: Work and employment play a uniquely powerful role in structuring people’s days and conferring a sense of belonging, status, and self-worth. Although AI boosters insist that people would organically find new sources of meaning if most jobs are automated, there is no reason to assume so.
snippet: Work and employment play a uniquely powerful role in structuring people’s days and conferring a sense of belonging, status, and self-worth. Although AI boosters insist that people would organically
creator: Carl Benedikt Frey
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: To Avoid Climate Peril, the World Must Electrify
content: The war in the Gulf has unleashed powerful market forces that the rest of the world can harness to accelerate electrification and keep the Paris climate agreement's primary objectives on track. Doing so will not only mitigate climate change, but also strengthen economic resilience worldwide.
snippet: The war in the Gulf has unleashed powerful market forces that the rest of the world can harness to accelerate electrification and keep the Paris climate agreement's primary objectives on track. Doing
creator: Murat Kurum
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What the Iran War Taught the World About Food Security
content: Perhaps the most unsettling lesson from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is the gap between what we knew and how we prepared. Countries should use whatever easing of immediate pressures occurs to strengthen resilience before the next shock arrives.
snippet: Perhaps the most unsettling lesson from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is the gap between what we knew and how we prepared. Countries should use whatever easing of immediate pressures occurs to
creator: Maximo Torero
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Orange Bandit
content: According to the Roman Emperor Tiberius, a ruler can either “shear the sheep” for many years, by encouraging productive activity from which to generate tax revenues, or he can “skin them alive” just once. By brazenly using the US government to pillage the country, President Donald Trump has apparently chosen the knife.
snippet: According to the Roman Emperor Tiberius, a ruler can either “shear the sheep” for many years, by encouraging productive activity from which to generate tax revenues, or he can “skin them alive” just
creator: Aziz Huq
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How (Not) to Conserve Tropical Forests
content: Although the Tropical Forest Forever Facility that emerged from the last United Nations Climate Conference is unlikely to succeed, critically important global conservation efforts are not doomed. Through sustainability-linked sovereign bonds and loans, tropical countries can overcome the TFFF’s fatal flaws.
snippet: Although the Tropical Forest Forever Facility that emerged from the last United Nations Climate Conference is unlikely to succeed, critically important global conservation efforts are not doomed.
creator: Bård Harstad
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page
